misconceptions.description: Uses aggregate (COUNT/SUM) but forgets GROUP BY.
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT name, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY name
misconceptions.remediation_hint: When using aggregates with other columns, use GROUP BY for non-aggregated columns.

misconceptions.description: Forgets ON condition in JOIN (Cartesian product).
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Always specify ON condition when joining tables.

misconceptions.description: Uses WHERE instead of HAVING for aggregate conditions.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT a, COUNT(*) FROM t WHERE COUNT(*) > 1 GROUP BY a
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT a, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY a HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Filter by aggregate results with HAVING, not WHERE.

misconceptions.description: Uses = NULL instead of IS NULL.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x = NULL
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE x IS NULL
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL for null checks.
Prefer listing columns for clarity and performance.

misconceptions.description: Uses wrong quotes for string literals in SQL.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Use single quotes for string literals in SQL.

misconceptions.description: Confuses column position and name in ORDER BY.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT a, b FROM t ORDER BY 0
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT a, b FROM t ORDER BY a
misconceptions.remediation_hint: ORDER BY uses column names or 1-based positions.

misconceptions.description: Uses aggregate in WHERE instead of subquery or HAVING.
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t GROUP BY id HAVING SUM(x) > 10
misconceptions.remediation_hint: WHERE cannot use aggregates; use HAVING after GROUP BY or a subquery.

misconceptions.description: INSERT without column list when values don't match table order.
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Specify column list in INSERT for clarity and correctness.

misconceptions.example_correct_code: UPDATE t SET x = 0 WHERE id = 1
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Always use WHERE in UPDATE unless you intend to update all rows.

misconceptions.description: Uses non-equality in JOIN ON or wrong columns.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id > b.id
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.a_id
misconceptions.remediation_hint: JOIN ON typically uses equality on key columns.

misconceptions.description: Uses GROUP BY when DISTINCT would suffice.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT name FROM t GROUP BY name
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Use DISTINCT for unique values; GROUP BY when aggregating.

misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY id LIMIT 5
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Use ORDER BY before LIMIT for predictable results.

misconceptions.description: Uses = with subquery that returns multiple rows.
misconceptions.example_incorrect_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = (SELECT id FROM u)
misconceptions.example_correct_code: SELECT * FROM t WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM u)
misconceptions.remediation_hint: Use IN for subqueries that return multiple rows.

misconceptions.description: Wrong CREATE TABLE syntax (missing parentheses or types).
